hooks.prompt: STOP. execute_blender_code is the FALLBACK. Check structured tools first: create_object, transform_object, manage_materials, manage_lights, set_camera, manage_world, mesh_operation, modify_object, build_node_graph, procedural_generate, manage_armature, set_keyframe, manage_physics, manage_collections, manage_hierarchy, render_scene, render_region, import_model, batch_execute. Only proceed if NO structured tool covers this operation.

If the result contains 'No Blender connected' or 'connection refused' or the tool errored: STOP and tell the user: You need the BlenderWeave Blender addon running. Make sure Blender is open with the addon enabled. If you don't have it installed: 1) Download blender_weave.zip from https://github.com/pedronaugusto/blender-weave/releases 2) In Blender: Edit > Preferences > Extensions > Install from Disk 3) Select the zip and enable BlenderWeave — it auto-connects. If the result contains VERIFY FAIL lines, stop and fix (use recursive=True for hierarchies). Otherwise continue normally.
